task_completion.description: The original object image is opened in the image editing software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface (or similar image editor) is visible with the object image loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Drop Shadow filter or tool is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Drop Shadow' dialog box is visible on the screen, typically accessed via the Filters -> Light and Shadow menu.

task_completion.description: A drop shadow effect is visibly applied to the object in the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The object in the image canvas now has a visible shadow behind or below it, showing a darker, semi-transparent area adjacent to the object.

task_completion.description: The export dialog is opened to save the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' or 'Save As' dialog box is visible on screen within the image editor.

The export path is explicitly set to /home/ga/Documents/output.png in the export dialog, followed by clicking the Export button, or a file manager shows output.png newly created in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image editor rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the image editor's GUI being clicked and manipulated, not just a terminal window running ImageMagick or similar CLI commands.

integrity.description: The drop shadow was applied using the software's built-in tools, not by manually painting black pixels.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Drop Shadow dialog/settings window is used, rather than the paintbrush tool or importing a separate pre-made shadow image.

integrity.description: The agent exported the file through the software's export function rather than just renaming an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog from the image editor is used to save the file, rather than terminal 'mv' commands or file manager renaming.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GIMP's Drop Shadow filter is located in Filters -> Light and Shadow -> Drop Shadow. A valid drop shadow should visibly decrease luminance in a 5-30% area adjacent to the object. The final image must be exported exactly to /home/ga/Documents/output.png.
